# Bugs тАФ work in progress

Living list of known, not-yet-fixed bugs in the dock conversation/listening
pipeline. Each entry: the **symptom** (what the user sees), what's **verified**
(asserted with file:line, I'm sure of it), what's **suspected** (a hypothesis
not yet reproduced), and the candidate **fix direction**. Don't treat suspected
lines as ground truth тАФ reproduce before fixing.

Status legend: ЁЯФ┤ open ┬╖ ЁЯЯб partial fix / mitigated ┬╖ ЁЯЯв fixed (move to an RCA in
`docs/rca/` when closed).

## BUG-1 тАФ UI shows transcription when NOT in listening mode ЁЯЯб

**Fix landed (not yet device-verified).** The user-speech caption is now GATED on
an open listening/followup window: the Subtitle shows transcript text only when
`convMode тИИ {listening, followup}` ([DockScreen.kt тАФ `listeningWindow`](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/ui/DockScreen.kt)),
so an ungated always-on-STT transcript (or a stale one after a missed
window-close frame) can't paint. Belt-and-suspenders: PerceptionWiring also
clears the endpointed `_transcript` when convMode leaves the window
([PerceptionWiring.renderConvMode](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/ui/face/PerceptionWiring.kt)),
mirroring the interim clear that already existed in RemoteBrain. **Restart angle:**
the gate makes the caption self-heal as soon as ANY non-listening frame arrives,
which is the common recovery тАФ but I did NOT add a phone-side caption heartbeat,
so the dropped-frame race in "suspected" below is mitigated, not provably
eliminated. Verify on device after a server restart mid-listening.

---

### Original analysis

**Symptom.** The subtitle band shows live transcript text on the face UI even
though the dock is *not* in listening mode. The dock always transcribes in the
background (server-side always-on STT); the bug is that this leaks onto the UI.
Intermittent тАФ happens *sometimes*, and seems correlated with restarts (app
restart or server/station restart). The inconsistency is itself the worry: it
points to a state/timing race, not a static condition.

**Verified (sure).** There are two distinct transcript paths, gated differently:

- **Interim (live partial) transcript** тАФ server-side, IS gated by listening.
  `setListeningResolver((dock) => session(dock).isListening())`
  ([brain/index.ts:336](../../orbit-station/server/src/modules/brain/index.ts#L336));
  the perception module won't fire interims unless the resolver returns true
  ([perception/index.ts:307,431-433](../../orbit-station/server/src/modules/perception/index.ts#L431)).
  On the phone, an interim is cleared when `convMode` leaves listening/followup
  ([RemoteBrain.kt:399](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/agent/RemoteBrain.kt#L399)).

- **Final transcript** тАФ the always-on server STT, one per endpointed utterance.
  This is **NOT gated by listening at all**. The phone renders the `perception`
  topic `"transcript"` frame for any non-blank text
  ([DockScreen.kt:199-203](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/ui/DockScreen.kt#L199-L203)),
  and the subtitle shows it whenever the text is non-blank тАФ the display
  condition checks **neither listening state nor mic state**:
  [DockScreen.kt:1129](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/ui/DockScreen.kt#L1129)
  is just `transcriptText.isNotBlank()`. The final transcript is only cleared on
  `onTurnSettled` ([DockScreen.kt:124](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/ui/DockScreen.kt#L124))
  or on a new tap ([PerceptionWiring.kt:179](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/ui/face/PerceptionWiring.kt#L179)).

So: a final transcript arriving outside a listening window (which is always
possible тАФ that path is ungated) renders on the UI and lingers until a turn
settles. That structural gap is **certain**.

**Suspected (not reproduced).** The *restart correlation*. Plausible mechanisms,
neither yet proven:
- After a server restart the brain's `isListening()` resolver / session state is
  briefly stale, so interims leak before state reconciles.
- A final transcript in flight at restart arrives and sticks because no
  turn-settle event ever fires to clear it.

**Fix direction.** Gate the transcript subtitle on the station's listening mode,
and clear the *final* transcript (not just the interim) on `convMode тЖТ idle`
(the interim already clears at RemoteBrain.kt:399; the final does not).
Reproduce the restart timing before claiming the restart angle is fixed.

---

## BUG-2 тАФ Mic-off doesn't reliably stop listening ЁЯЯв

**Fixed + verified server-side (v24 / 2026-06-26).** Mic-off now propagates to the
station AND is guarded locally:
- Phone reports the toggle: `RemoteBrain.sendMicMuted` тЖТ `mic-muted` agent frame,
  fired from the existing `LaunchedEffect(micGranted, micMuted)` in DockScreen.
- Station closes the window + blocks new ones: `mic-muted` тЖТ
  `session.setMicMuted` тЖТ `ConversationState.setMuted`. While muted, an open
  listening/followup window drops to `idle` (emitted back to the phone) and
  `tap`/`tapOpen`/`faceArrival` are inert (vad/utterance already require an open
  window). Unmute lifts the gate without auto-reopening (tap to talk again).
- Local guard: `PerceptionWiring.renderConvMode` refuses the listening glow while
  `micMuted`, so a late/racing listening frame can't flip the face back.
Covered by unit tests: `conversation-state.test.ts` (mute closes/blocks/reopens)
and `PerceptionWiringTransitionTest.kt` (muted refuses glow; unmute listens
again).

#### Review Finding 1 (HIGH) тАФ muting *mid-reply* didn't actually mute

An adversarial review of the first fix caught a real hole: `setMuted` only closed
the window from `listening`/`followup`. If the user muted while a turn was active
(`thinking`/`speaking`), the window wasn't closed; the reply then finished and
`speakEnd` re-opened a `followup` listening window with **no mute check**. Because
the WebRTC mic is NOT actually muted on mute (only the local pipeline stops тАФ
`PerceptionService.stop`), audio kept reaching the station, so that re-opened
window could paint a caption AND **fire a live agent turn while the user believed
the mic was off** тАФ a privacy bug, not cosmetic.

Closed at the authoritative chokepoints in `conversation-state.ts`:
- `isListening()` returns false while muted (gates BOTH the interim caption тАФ
  `index.ts setListeningResolver` тАФ and turn admission).
- `utteranceEnded()` returns not-addressed while muted (no spoken utterance becomes
  a turn, regardless of how a window opened).
- `speakEnd()` goes to `idle` (not `followup`) while muted (fixes the STATE, emits
  an idle frame to the phone), with a matching guard in `#prune`'s speak-timeout.
- Phone: `PerceptionWiring` now re-applies the guard on a `micMuted` EDGE (not only
  on a `convMode` frame), so a mute right after a `listening` frame drops the glow
  immediately instead of waiting for the station's idle frame.

Regression tests added: mute-while-speaking тЖТ idle-not-followup, mute-while-thinking,
muted-utterance-not-addressed.

#### Verification (server-side, live, headless тАФ no adb)

Driven against the running station via `POST /api/brain/:dock/debug/event` (the
headless conversation-event endpoint; `mic-muted`/`mic-unmuted` events added there
for this). dock-redmi was on v24 (all fixes installed); these exercised the real
session + conversation-state:
1. **Basic mute** тАФ tapтЖТlistening, mic-mutedтЖТ**idle** тЬУ
2. **Mute mid-reply (Finding 1)** тАФ mute while speaking тЖТ tts-endтЖТ**idle, NOT
   followup** тЬУ; utterance while muted тЖТ **not addressed** тЬУ
3. **Unmute restores** тАФ muted tap inert тЬУ; unmute no auto-open тЬУ; tapтЖТlistening,
   utteranceтЖТaddressed тЬУ

**Still needs device eyes (visual, not server-observable):** the face glow dropping
on mute, the "waking up while still listening" UX, and the toggle's TOUCH reliability
(see Tap-reactivity below тАФ the touch issue WAS addressed there, separately).

---

### Original analysis

**Symptom.** Toggling mic OFF in the UI doesn't reliably stop listening. It can
enter listening state anyway, and shows "waking up" while still listening.
Mic-off should mean NOT listening at all.

**Verified (sure).** `toggleMic()` тЖТ `setMicMuted()` тЖТ `syncPrivacyAndSpeaker()`
([FaceController.kt:216-244](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/ui/face/FaceController.kt#L216-L244))
does three purely **local** things: sets `_micMuted`, forces the face to `Idle`,
sets the speaker mute pip. It **never sends anything to the station.**

The station is the sole owner of listening
([PerceptionWiring.kt:81-126](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/ui/face/PerceptionWiring.kt#L81-L126)).
When it emits `conversation: "listening"`, `renderConvMode()` calls
`controller.listen()` and drives the face back to `Listening` тАФ **overriding the
`Idle` mic-off just set, with no check of `_micMuted`**. That's the "switched
mic off but it goes into listening anyway."

The local perception-service stop on mic-mute
([DockScreen.kt:574-577](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/ui/DockScreen.kt#L574-L577))
is local-only and does **not** stop the server-side always-on STT (it runs off
the media stream, server-side), so the station keeps transcribing and can
re-enter listening even with the local mic muted тЖТ "mic off, still listening /
still transcribing."

The "waking up" text is `pipelineStatus` rendered via the `Idle` hint
`"tap to talk ┬╖ $pipelineStatus"`
([DockScreen.kt:1183](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/ui/DockScreen.kt#L1183),
fed by [PerceptionWiring.kt:268-272](../../node-dock/app/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/orbit/dock/ui/face/PerceptionWiring.kt#L268-L272)).

**Fix direction.** Mic-off must send a stop/mute signal UP to the station so it
stops the listening window (and ideally pauses server STT for that dock), and
`renderConvMode()` should respect local mute (don't drive into `Listening` while
`_micMuted`).

---

## Shared root cause

Both bugs come from the same seam: **mic-off and the listening-window display
are local UI state, but listening + transcription are owned by the station.**
The mic toggle has no upstream signal to tell the station "stop / I'm muted,"
and the transcript subtitle has no gate on the station's listening mode.

---

## BUG-3 тАФ Tap reactivity: mic toggle + tap-to-listen ЁЯЯв

**Symptom.** The mic toggle in the UI needed several taps to register; and
tap-to-listen felt unreliable in part of the screen. The user's diagnosis (right):
not hardware тАФ the tap hit-areas.

**Two distinct causes, both fixed (v24).**
1. **Mic/cam toggle hit target too small** тАФ `StatusBar.ToggleIcon` had only 3dp
   vertical padding (~20dp tall touch area, under Android's 48dp minimum). The
   clickable is now wrapped in a `sizeIn(minWidth = 48.dp, minHeight = 48.dp)` Box;
   the visible chip is unchanged, the touch area meets the minimum. (Side effect:
   the bottom status bar is ~48dp tall now тАФ acceptable; the bottom edge is the
   hardest place to hit, so a bigger target there is a feature.)
2. **Left ~280dp was a tap dead-zone for tap-to-listen** тАФ `EventLog` is a 280dp
   full-height `LazyColumn` that consumed pointer events across its area even with
   nothing to scroll, so taps there never reached the screen-wide
   `detectTapGestures` (the listen toggle). Set `userScrollEnabled = false` (it
   scrolls programmatically via `animateScrollToItem`, so auto-tail still works);
   taps now fall through to the listen gesture.

Also fixed alongside: the **"waking upтАж" pill** showed on mic-OFF too
(`micGranted && !perceptionReady`), because muting stops the local pipeline so
`perceptionReady` goes false тАФ misread as "still coming up" when the user had
deliberately turned the mic off. Now gated on `!micMuted`.

**Verify on device:** both hit areas, and that the pill no longer shows when muted.

---

## Feature тАФ tap the build number to force an OTA update-check ЁЯЯв

Not a bug тАФ a UX gap surfaced while shipping these fixes. The app is a passive OTA
client: it waits for the station to push an `ota/available` offer (on build, or on
peer-join/heartbeat re-announce). If a dock was attached to an earlier server
instance when a build's one-shot announce fired, it could sit "behind" with no
visible way to re-check.

**Added (v24):** tapping the version/build label (top-start) publishes an
`ota/check` frame carrying the dock's current build; the station's OTA module
(`ota/index.ts`) replies with a directed `available` offer iff this dock is behind
(same gate as the peer-join re-announce, directed at the requester). Long-press on
the same label still flags feedback (unchanged). Shows a "Checking for updateтАж"
toast. **Verify on device:** tap behind тЖТ offer appears; tap when up-to-date тЖТ
toast, no offer.
